preamble: The debuccalization of the lateral affricate [tɬ] into the glottal fricative [h] is a radical form of lenition, where all oral articulation features are lost, leaving only the glottal state.

examples.note: Common in some central dialects of Nahuatl, where final [tɬ] or intervocalic [tɬ] can weaken into [h].
Extreme cases of lateral affricate weakening under specific stress or position constraints.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
